$schema_note: Misconception suggestions harvested by MisconceptionAnalyst from common_miss prose in data/buckets/tense_choice.json. PENDING architecture ratification into data/misconceptions.json. Pass 2 (formation + usage trees). Many misses REUSE specifics defined in pass 1 or the v1 registry (cross-tense/cross-kind reuse is the point); only genuinely new specifics are defined here, the rest listed under reuses_existing. Thread: inter_chat/Architecture_MisconceptionAnalyst_registry_harvest.md (see v2).

proposed_definition.label: Tense / aspect / mood selection
proposed_definition.description: Choosing the wrong tense, aspect, or mood where the chosen form would itself be correctly built. The selection counterpart to the formation families (which are about mis-building a form). Cross-kind: aspect, sequence-of-tenses, and mood-trigger selection recur across every tense pairing. OPEN_QUESTIONS already anticipated tense choice as a cross-kind discrimination misconception.

proposed_family_actions.note: The headline pass-2 proposal. ~30 misses across the tense_choice and imperfect-usage/discrimination trees collapse here. Distinct from the formation families and from transfer (though many discrimination errors are transfer-driven and could co-tag).

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Progressive over-used for the simple tense
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Using the stare + gerundio progressive where the simple present/imperfect is the natural choice, typically by mapping every English -ing form onto a progressive.
examples: sto mangiando for the habitual mangio
stavo mangiando for the descriptive mangiavo
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: One pattern over both the present and past progressive buckets.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Choosing the morphological future where a present-for-future is natural, or the present where the future is required (distant, uncertain, probabilistic).
examples: morphological future where present-for-future is more natural
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Bidirectional selection error; one specific, both directions.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Wrong tense/mood combination for a counterfactual
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Mis-selecting the verb forms of a counterfactual conditional: using present indicative + future (a real-condition pattern) for a counterfactual, or judging the accepted colloquial double-imperfect as wrong.
examples: se ho tempo, verro' (real-condition pattern) for a counterfactual
treating the colloquial imperfect counterfactual as an error

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Indicative chosen where a trigger requires the subjunctive
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Leaving the verb in the indicative after a trigger that requires the subjunctive (opinion, emotion, hypothetical conjunctions, negation). The SELECTION error, distinct from mis-forming the subjunctive (see mood.indicative_for_subjunctive_form).
examples: penso che e' (for penso che sia)
sono contento che sei qui (for che tu sia qui)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: FLAGGED PAIR: choice (here) vs form (mood.indicative_for_subjunctive_form in congiuntivo). Same surface, different diagnosis; keep both so the drill-down can separate 'didn't know it was triggered' from 'couldn't build it'.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Four trigger buckets, one selection pattern.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Using the imperfect for a clearly completed prior-to-prior event, or the trapassato for an ongoing background state.
examples: avevo mangiato quando sei entrato (implies finished) where ongoing was meant
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Aspect selection between two past tenses.
